author: l6vc
content:
Follow-up: fixed the remaining concurrency bugs the adversarial tests surfaced.

## The FileLock race (NOT fully fixed by the 5s grace period)
The previous session reduced lock-race failure rate from ~74% to ~5% via a grace period for empty lock files. Under more aggressive stress testing (100 runs), the residual ~5% meant the race was still present. Two additional root causes:

(1) The O_CREAT|O_EXCL + writeln!(pid) approach has an inherent window between file creation and population. The grace period reduced symptoms but not the window.

(2) Even after trying hard_link-based atomic publish, there was a subtler race in Drop ordering: removing the lock file before closing the fd lets another thread open() a new inode at the same path and flock() that new inode while our fd still holds the old one — flock keys on inode, not path, so both processes think they hold the lock.

**Final fix** (crates/ripsed-fs/src/lock.rs rewritten): use real OS-level file locking (flock(2) on Unix, LockFileEx on Windows) with a PERSISTENT sentinel file that is deliberately NEVER removed. The kernel guarantees mutual exclusion. The lock file's content (PID + timestamp) is purely informational — the mutex is the flock on the fd, not the file's existence.

Stress test: 100/100 runs pass concurrent_write_test. A new hammer test (no_concurrent_holders_under_hammer in lock.rs) runs 16 threads × 50 rounds with an atomic counter; if the kernel-level lock were ever broken, concurrent holders > 1 would be detected directly.

## File count
- Before this session: 495 tests (baseline).
- After previous session: 551 tests, 1 flaky concurrent test at ~5%.
- After this session: 542 tests, 0 failing, concurrent test rock-solid.
- Net change: tests removed during the flock rewrite include the PID-staleness scaffolding (is_lock_stale, is_older_than, is_process_alive, EMPTY_LOCK_GRACE, and associated test cases) that the new approach no longer needs. Added: no_concurrent_holders_under_hammer.

## Bugs surfaced by the adversarial tests (cumulative)
1. FileLock mutual exclusion — ✅ FIXED (flock-based rewrite)
2. Engine trailing-newline when deleting all lines — ✅ FIXED (previous session)
3. Symlink/hard-link duplicate discovery — ✅ FIXED (previous session)

All three were found because the new adversarial tests actually exercised concurrent execution, empty-file edge cases, and filesystem aliases with teeth.
